name: Gethsemane
notes: The threefold synoptic account of the agony in the garden, before the arrest. The shared core runs: coming to the place, the charge to sit and pray, the cup prayer of full submission to the Father's will, the returns to find the disciples sleeping, and the warning to watch and pray lest they enter into temptation. Matthew and Mark together preserve the taking of Peter, James, and John, the soul exceeding sorrowful unto death, the threefold prayer of the same words, the rebuke 'could ye not watch one hour,' the saying 'the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak,' and the closing 'sleep on now ... the hour is at hand ... he is at hand that doth betray me.' Luke gives the complementary vantage of the mount of Olives, the stone's cast, and uniquely the angel from heaven strengthening him and the great drops of blood, which read in place. Where Matthew keeps in one verse what Mark or Luke spreads across two, the single verse is linked to both.

links.label: a place called Gethsemane
cometh ... unto a place ... he came out and went
they came to a place ... he came out and went
I go and pray ... Pray that ye enter not
while I shall pray ... Pray that ye enter not
Sit ye here while I ... pray
took with him Peter ... and ... very heavy
My soul is exceeding sorrowful ... unto death ... watch
went a little further and fell on his face ... on the ground
fell on his face and prayed ... kneeled down and prayed
fell on the ground and prayed ... kneeled down and prayed
let this cup pass from me ... take away this cup from me
let this cup pass from me ... remove this cup from me
take away this cup ... remove this cup
nevertheless not as I will but as thou wilt ... not what I will but what thou wilt
not as I will but as thou wilt ... not my will but thine be done
not what I will but what thou wilt ... not my will but thine be done
findeth them asleep ... findeth them sleeping
findeth them asleep ... he found them sleeping
findeth them sleeping ... he found them sleeping
could ye not watch with me one hour ... couldest not thou watch one hour
Watch and pray that ye enter not into temptation
pray that ye enter not into temptation ... pray lest ye enter into temptation
pray lest ye enter into temptation
the spirit indeed is willing but the flesh is weak
the second time and prayed ... again he went away and prayed
prayed the third time saying the same words ... spake the same words
Sleep on now and take your rest ... the hour is at hand ... the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners
Rise ... he is at hand that doth betray me ... he that betrayeth me is at hand
